item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Thursday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
eh
i guess
bad videos too
great so far
sort of
Dr. John C. Rumble
mmm hmm
he has a point
do they still do this?
oh shit
more power to you gentlemen i guess
simpsons thing
who doesn't?
as is tradition
i admire dylan's honesty
uh oh
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Saw and wanted to share
item.description: I needed to share something wholesome
item.dc:creator: cynicaloldman

item.title: One of musk’s SpaceX starships just blew up over Turks & Caicos. SpaceX is calling it a “rapid unscheduled disassembly “
item.description: Apparently a lot of commercial flights were forced to take evasive action, turning, holding, diverting, etc. to avoid falling debris from SpaceX's failed Starship. I hope no one was hit by any debris!
item.dc:creator: SisterMidnight

item.title: The idea that a bunch of Americans flooding Rednote is exposing the Chinese workforce to the concepts of weekends and unionization is extremely funny to me
item.dc:creator: MicroChannelMaster

item.title: Painted myself as Mary Jane - Iron Spider - MCroft07
item.description: Saw a few comic covers of Mary Jane as the Iron Spider and so I thought I'd play around with the design! At the end, I wish I had filmed against a green screen, but I was a little too tired and just forgot so it made it a lot harder to animate in the iron legs. However, I'm still learning, so it doesn't bother me :) Just trying my best. Thanks for all the nice comments, it's appreciated. Now for the big whammy faq Faq: I use body paint and do these in a mirror It's from some Marvel comic covers, I didn't copy them exactly because I like to add in my own style. No, it is not AI Yes, I have read comic books in my life. No, I will not list every comic book ever existed to prove it to you (are you ok?) I use professional body paint covers this means I am not wearing a top but I am also not naked. I paint myself, I make the props myself, I film myself, and I also edit. This is my full time thing now (yayy) I paint these by looking in a mirror, with brushes, and paint It takes between 6-12 hours each Body painting myself is my hobby/passion I do own clothes “Why don’t you just wear clothes” once again, body painting myself is my hobby/passion. I am painting my skin, I am not wearing a suit - but that does not mean I am naked. I know, even I think this is a ridiculous set of questions I have to constantly answer I did not go to art school, I am self taught. Talent gets you so far, but this is a learned skill. I've been painting myself for 16 years. The 07 does not mean I was born in 2007, UK 7 is my shoe size LMAO No, I don't need help painting my back, I don't want to paint my back, I don't need to paint my back. (also gross) No, I don't need help washing it off. (also you are gross) If you want to see more I am MCroft07 on IG, twitter, Tiktok, YouTube I paint them live on twitch I like doing the lip syncs, they're fun for me while I paint them. I couldn’t care less if you think they’re cringe. I am cringe but I am free My tattoos on my ribs are from Tomb Raider - I am a big TR fan I do not have an OF but if you want to send me money THAT badly you can just tip my twitch account. Haha. The shower afterwards takes about 15 minutes, I use a regular sponge and shower gel. The reason why it can look different at the end is because I am wearing a wig, I promise you it is not a different person because the hair changed. While on this topic, if my eyes change colour it’s because I am wearing contact lenses. (Those with an IQ above 10… I know. Even I’m shocked the wig thing is a frequent comment). I use body paint. I do not know where you can buy body paint in every country. I know in the UK it is available at FacePaintingshop.com. I use a plethora of different brands, it’s best to search for them yourself online. I don’t work in Hollywood, I don’t want to. I just want to improve my body painting for myself. I am English, not American. (not sure why this upset some people though??) I do not go outside like this to scare people. 1, that would be very cold. 2, in this current age, everyone would take photos without me knowing and it would likely dox me :)))))) Thank you to those of you who came from Imgur to my Twitch channel! “What’s the point in painting yourself when you can wear clothes?” all my clothes are in the laundry, had to resort to this, tough life It would not be quicker to jump into a pool of paint - think of the drying time?! If you're still here I’m currently eating salt and vinegar crisps from aldi I don’t care that I wash it off at the end, I get to reuse this canvas, we love a reduce, reuse, recycle queen. I can promise you there is not *a man* painting these and I am pretending to paint myself. I am genuinely painting myself. If you refuse to believe me, please seek help.
item.dc:creator: MCroft07

item.title: By request Dads being Dads ( and grandads) part 2 ( now with uncles)
item.description:
He's afraid so all the guys sing to make him forget he's scared. Lovely
It's the small acts of love and kindness that makes us feel seen and cared for.
I'm not crying, you're just blurry.
Is this cheating? Nope, judges say it's fair play.
His wife suffered a brain injury with a poor prognosis. He never gives up on her. Why are you still blurry?
Just a dad that i really like. He deserves his applause ?
The Dad's who didn't want a dog, to be fair how can anyone resist dog cuddles?
Dads relaxes in action
Dad is off to a great start. Hope yinz enjoyed this dump of male positivity. Feel free to share your funny or wholesome dad stories in the comments.
item.dc:creator: indigogogogo

item.title: Discworld reading order - a small collection of infographics
item.description:
I looked up the lists as an introduction for someone else, then fiddled with the first one to make it a one-pager, and thought they might be useful for others as well. Then I added the others, because when looking for the scroll version, I found a bunch of other fun variations, and I love me a good infographic. I really enjoy the different approaches and styles on the ones I've collected here. The original versions of the lists above are from here (as pdfs): https://www.terrypratchettbooks.com/discworld-reading-order/
This link likely not the actual origin of this scroll-style graphic, but it's where I found a high-res file that also wasn't cropped to shreds: https://epl.bibliocommons.com/list/share/1400140557/1457082847
https://www.epicreads.com/blog/the-official-discworld-infographic/
I'm glad to have found a high-quality version of this, but the creator seems unknown. The closest I got was a Facebook-entry from 2017, that said it was made by a reddit user. I searched there in various ways, but was not successful. The entry with the above version, including a discussion on perceived errors of the graphic, is here: https://www.facebook.com/photo.php?fbid=686297958229609&id=379335908925817&set=a.380457152147026
The link is to the Tumblr of the chart creator, but I have no idea where the origin-post itself is. Tumblr seems messy, and the chart was a random (and fun) web-discovery for me. https://goddamnshinyrock.tumblr.com/
Found no original source on this one, sorry. Nor on the one below.
This graph is fun because it appears to be the early version of many of the reading graphs that are around today, including the scroll-style further up by the same author (Krzysztof K. Kietzman). Old enough to not include the later Tiffany Aching books – though probably not as old as its early-Windows-era graphic style suggests. No shade; I dig that for the nostalgia factor alone. But wow, the author seriously upped their game later on.
item.dc:creator: abetteridiot

item.title: Starship breaks in front of an airplane
item.dc:creator: Razade

item.title: Cake day mental health dump!
item.description:
I save mental health memes to send to my son.
You may remember him from his cross country bike trip that went viral here a few years ago. He had had a suicidal episode and cycling from coast to coast was the medicine he decided he needed. And it was a great trip and an amazing adventure.
But things got bad again after he came home. He couldn't sustain the mental health gains that came with all the exercise and novelty of the trip.
He struggled along for a while and began to plan for another trip to have something to look forward to. But then things got a lot worse and eventually he had a major crisis and was hospitalized again. He spent Christmas in the psych ward a year ago.
But, he was finally diagnosed with bipolar I and understanding this illness was helpful for all of us.
This is a mental illness that usually requires medication management, but after some trials and bad side effects he decided he to go back to his "factory settings" and learn to manage it himself. This worries me, of course, but he's an adult and making his own decisions and it's not up to me. All I can do is love, educate, and support him the best I can without enabling unhealthy behaviors.
He decided eventually to do the next bike trip he'd been deaming of and he set off last May. This time he took a train to Seattle then biked toward Colorado, seeing a lot of the national parks on the way. He did not expect to come back from this trip, but rather planned to find his place in the world and figure himself out on his own. Again, this worries me, but he is fiercely independent and has to do things his way.
So, I support the best I can from afar and send him mental health memes.
Anyway, he did make it to Utah when his money ran too low for comfort, and decided to get a job and make a life there for himself. He's made some significant strides and I'm proud of him.
He has even started to make friends through DM ing a dungeons and dragons campaign... An old passion of his. This is a huge deal for him after significant social rejection and isolation! One day, on his way to d&d I sent him this next one:
And he sent me this:
And it made me cry. Those "I love you" texts are very precious to moms of young adult children, let me tell you.
Here's a rainbow I saw after one rainy Renaissance festival day when I was vending. It seemed appropriate to put right here!
I do hope he'll get back into therapy, but right now forming social connections and doing what he's passionate about seems to be pretty good medicine.
He is now in the process of applying to a career program that will provide housing, food and training for free, which is amazing. He's had a lot of trouble finding a long term housing situation out there. Check out Job Corp near you if you're in the US and need a leg up. He's planning to study computer repair or IT there. Fingers crossed!
This is what I remind myself when he doesn't call me back. Sigh.
And here's a chicken tax for you! Her name is Poppy the House Chicken and she lives with me now... My other son found her while on his own road trip and brought her home for me. It's too cold to set up and coop and get her some friends now, so she lives in the house. She's a good bird, though and I love her.
item.dc:creator: PandoraZora

item.title: Today was not a good day.
item.description: Not only was I supposed to let him say anything he wants to me, but she decided to tell me off in the middle of a meeting with my entire Special Education department. MV Edit: Thanks for listening, guys! I know this is my choice --I picked a tough career. I've been working with this kid for all three years of middle school (I'm his case manager) and he's made great behavioral progress, but this does not make what was said to me okay. Moving forward, I'll be putting, 'I would have sent (student) to the office, but, under the advisement of our counseling department, it would escalate him/her/them' in all of my restorative documentation. My incredibly supportive principal will see it each time. The counselor will be explaining herself soon enough.
item.dc:creator: GratingBobShrillPants
